_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX state-legislature batch: steve-toth, nathan-johnson, will-metcalf, joan-huffman, stan-gerdes. Evidence-first; every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence; never inferred from party. Includes candidacy-currency fixes (Toth won TX-02 GOP primary; Johnson & Huffman are 2026 statewide AG candidates).

set.office: Texas State Representative (HD-15) · 2026 R Nominee for U.S. House TX-02 (defeated Crenshaw 57-39 in 3/3/26 GOP primary; vs Shaun Finnie (D) Nov 2026)

profile.religion: Christian — ordained minister; former family-life pastor at The Woodlands Church; teaching elder at WoodsEdge Community Church
profile.confidence_note: Scored from TX House voting record + authored bills. TX-02 primary win web-verified 2026-06-19.

steve-toth@TX.notes_set: Among the most conservative TX House members (Rice study #4/150; YCT #1, 88th). Texas Right to Life 100%; Texas Alliance for Life 85% (89th). Author HB 3979 (CRT ban) and HB 1076 (Firearms Protection Act, criminalizing state enforcement of federal gun rules). Voted YES on SB 2 ESA voucher (2025) and HB 1927 permitless carry (2021). Authored HB 241 forensic-audit bill. No cross-party (left) deviations found; unscored cells are genuine evidence gaps, not contrary positions.

4.note: Author HB 3979 (CRT ban) + SB 8 work opposing LGBTQ ideology in schools

set.office: Texas State Senator (SD-16) · 2026 Democratic Nominee for TX Attorney General

profile.confidence_note: Scored from TX Senate voting record, authored bills, and single-issue endorsements. SD-16 seat not up in 2026; won Democratic AG runoff 5/26/2026 (web-verified 2026-06-19).

nathan-johnson@TX.notes_set: Democrat; consistent left-of-rubric record (Planned Parenthood-endorsed 2022/2024; authored 5 abortion-access bills; Everytown/Moms Demand Action endorsed; NO on SB 2 vouchers; floor opposition to SB 10 Ten Commandments & SB 11 school prayer). CREDIT WHERE EARNED — documented conservative cross-party deviation: voted YES on SJR 1 (2025) restricting pretrial bail for undocumented immigrants charged with felonies. Not up for Senate reelection in 2026; is the Democratic nominee for TX Attorney General.

will-metcalf@TX.notes_set: Conroe Republican; Texas Alliance for Life 100% and Texas Values Action 'Faith & Family Champion' 100% (2025). Co-authored Heartbeat Ban + Trigger Ban, HB 2012 ('In God We Trust' classroom displays), Constitutional Carry. Voted for SB 2 ESA. Endorsed by CLEAT/TMPA and NRA/TSRA. DOCUMENTED FISCAL DEVIATION: 'F' on the Texans for Fiscal Responsibility 2023 Fiscal Index. Intra-party flags (not rubric cells): voted AYE to impeach AG Paxton; backed Burrows for Speaker; censured by Montgomery County GOP (2025).

set.office: Texas State Senator (SD-17) · 2026 Republican candidate for TX Attorney General

profile.religion: Christian — 'Raised Catholic; Jesus Christ is my Lord and Savior' (self-stated, iVoterGuide)
profile.confidence_note: Scored from recorded votes, authored bills, and iVoterGuide self-reported survey answers. SD-17 seat not up in 2026; running for TX Attorney General (announced 6/23/2025; web-verified 2026-06-19).

joan-huffman@TX.notes_set: Senate Finance Chair; Texas Alliance for Life 100% (89th). Authored SB 3 border-wall appropriation; long-time author of TX bail reform (Damon Allen Act); co-sponsor of SB 1 (2021) election-integrity law; voted SB 4 (2017) sanctuary ban; voted YES on SB 2 ESA. iVoterGuide survey: pro-life, marriage one-man-one-woman, immutable sex, religious liberty, pro-2A. DOCUMENTED CENTRIST FLAGS (brand 'tell people what to skip'): YCT 88th rated her among the 5 LEAST-conservative Senate Republicans (score 72); Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career C+ with F years (2019/2021/2023); initially opposed HB 1927 constitutional carry as 'really poor public policy' before voting YES on final passage.

Won 2026 GOP primary (3/3/2026 over Tom Glass); faces Mary Klenz (D) Nov 2026 (web-verified 2026-06-19). First elected 2022; thinner record — many cells null for lack of evidence.

stan-gerdes@TX.notes_set: Bastrop-area Republican (first elected 2022). Co-author SB 14 (minor gender-procedure ban); author HB 4814 ('FURRIES Act', failed in committee). Voted YES on SB 2 ESA/voucher. NRA lifetime member; NRA/TSRA, Texas Right to Life & Texas Alliance for Life endorsed. TFR Fiscal Index 2025 = 71 (career grade 'F'). No cross-party (left) deviations found. Christian-liberty category entirely null — no documented positions located.
